channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Court orders Biden admin to stop selling border wall materials, was 'illegally subverting' laws: Texas AG
item.description: The Biden administration has been ordered to stop selling off border materials ahead of the incoming Trump administration next month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden White House to send $1.25 billion in weapon aid to Ukraine before Trump transition: report
item.description: The Biden White House is set to announce an additional $1.25 billion in military assistance to Ukraine as the incoming Trump administration to take office in January.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans gain edge in Minnesota House after DFLer drops out over residency case
item.description: Republicans gain one-seat majority heading into 2025 session as a DFL-won seat becomes vacant. A special election will come early next year.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Requiring federal workers to return to office will be hard: Comer
item.description: U.S. Rep. James Comer believes evaluating the federal workforce's efficiency is critical to eliminating federal waste.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Trump 'border czar' says he will override sanctuary city roadblocks
item.description: "Sanctuary jurisdictions aren't going to stop what we're going to do," Tom Homan said in a NewsNation interview.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: Congressional lawmakers almost got a pay bump this year. Then it crashed and burned
item.description: Lawmakers on Capitol Hill haven’t had a pay increase since 2009, representing a 31 percent decline in their salary when adjusted for inflation.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt, MPR News

item.title: Hochul signs bill that will charge oil and gas firms $75B, but critics say customers will really foot the tab
item.description: New York Governor Kathy Hochul signed legislation that will issue assessments to energy firms operating in New York; funds will be used for climate mitigation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The US says it pushed retraction of a famine warning for north Gaza. Aid groups express concern
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A lead organization monitoring for food crises around the world withdrew a new report this week warning of imminent famine in north Gaza under what it called Israel's “near-total blockade,” after the U.S. asked for its retraction, U.S. officials told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: President Biden signs 50 bills into law
item.description: Before leaving to spend the rest of the holiday in the Virgin Islands, President Joe Biden signed 50 bills into law on Christmas Eve, as his time in office comes to an end.
item.dc:creator: Traci Caldwell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vivian Moreno launches bid for Vargas' seat on Board of Supervisors
item.description: Days after San Diego County Board of Supervisors Chair Nora Vargas' surprise decision to leave her post at the end of her current term, the field for a potential special election to fill her seat has started to take shape.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Minnesota Democratic party chair says his wins could help nationally after loss to Trump
item.description: Ken Martin helped turn around the Minnesota Democratic-Farmer-Labor Party's finances and secure a streak of statewide wins for the party over 18 years. Now, he wants to do the same thing for the Democratic National Committee.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump tells 37 people with sentences commuted by Biden to 'go to hell' in lengthy Christmas post
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday used a lengthy Christmas Day social media post to tell the 37 people who had their death sentences commuted by President Biden to "go to hell.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fate of Trump's Cabinet picks unclear as Republicans prepare to take power in Senate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The fate of President-elect Donald Trump’s Cabinet is still unclear after Republican senators spent much of December carefully dodging questions about Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump faces roadblocks to ambitious border crackdown
item.description: President-elect Trump has made ambitious promises on the border and immigration, including the biggest deportation operation ever seen. But much like in Trump’s first term in office, his immigration agenda faces significant roadblocks.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden takes heat for vetoing bill that would add new judgeships
item.description: Federal judiciary leadership and some lawmakers are criticizing President Biden for vetoing a bill that would’ve added 66 new federal judgeships amid growing caseloads.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Trump vows to direct DOJ to pursue death penalty in wake of Biden clemencies
item.description: President-elect Trump vowed Tuesday to direct his Department of Justice (DOJ) to pursue the death penalty in the wake of President Biden’s clemencies of nearly every federal death row inmate.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Opinion: Elon Musk wants to ‘delete’ many Americans' financial lifeline
item.description: It is especially surprising that one of the first federal agencies to come under scrutiny from the incoming administration is one that has returned billions of dollars to many of the same consumers who were counting on leaders in Washington to look out for their wallets.
item.dc:creator: Sharon McGowan, opinion contributor, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bill Clinton discharged after hospitalization for flu
item.description: Former President Clinton was discharged Tuesday morning after being hospitalized a day earlier for flu, according to his spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Inside the Gaetz ethics report, a trove of new details alleging payments for sex and drug use
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House Ethics Committee's long-awaited report on Matt Gaetz documents a trove of salacious allegations, including sex with an underage girl, that tanked the Florida Republican's bid to lead the Justice Department.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill Clinton is hospitalized with a fever but in good spirits, spokesperson says
item.description: The 78-year-old former president was hospitalized “for testing and observation” after developing a fever, his deputy chief of staff said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Gaetz Ethics report finds ‘substantial evidence’ of statutory rape, drug use
item.description: The House Ethics Committee found “substantial evidence” that former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) paid a 17-year-old high school student for sex when he was 35, used illicit drugs like cocaine and ecstasy, and obstructed Congress’s investigation into his conduct.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump: Panama will lose control of canal if it continues to 'rip off' US
item.description: President-elect Trump took issue with fees charged to use the Panama Canal, a vital shipping route, on Saturday night, claiming that he will demand oversight of the waterway be given back to the U.S. when he returns to office if it's not changed.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, NewsNation

item.title: As Texas Chief Justice Nathan Hecht prepares to retire, he reflects on the Supreme Court he helped change
item.description: Over 35 years, Hecht modernized the court, increased access to justice for the poor and saw his conservative views come to dominate the bench.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Eleanor Klibanoff, KPRC 2
